column2: P0 test cases
column3: Expected results

column2: Sign in with a valid work email and correct password for an approved, active account
column3: User reaches the post-login home (or dashboard) and stays signed in; profile/org context loads normally.

column2: Sign in with a valid work email but an incorrect password
column3: Login is denied; user sees a single clear error that does not confirm whether the email exists; password field can be cleared or corrected without breaking the page.

column2: Sign in with an email address that was never registered
column3: Login is denied with the same type of credential error as a wrong password (no hint that the account is missing).

column2: Leave the email field empty and attempt to sign in
column3: Login is blocked; user sees a validation message asking for email (or equivalent) before authentication is attempted.

column2: Leave the password field empty and attempt to sign in
column3: Login is blocked; user sees a validation message asking for password before authentication is attempted.

column2: Sign in using an account that has been removed or deactivated by the organization
column3: Login is denied; message is safe and generic (no internal reason exposed); user is not signed in.

column2: Sign in using an account that was never approved or was explicitly rejected during onboarding
column3: Login is denied with a message that the account request was rejected (or cannot sign in); no access to the product.

column2: Sign in using an email that only ever used “Sign in with Google” (no product password set)
column3: Login is denied with guidance to use Google sign-in (or the correct method); user is not signed in with password-only flow.

column2: Sign in when the organization has turned on two-factor authentication for the user: enter correct password first
column3: User is prompted for the second step (OTP/app) before entering the app; after password alone, user is not fully inside the product until 2FA succeeds.

column2: Rapidly submit many sign-in attempts from the same browser/session in a short time
column3: After reasonable repetition, further attempts are temporarily blocked or throttled with a clear “try again later” style message; app remains stable and no silent failures.

column2: Create a job with all mandatory fields filled with valid data
column3: Job is created successfully and appears in the job list with correct title, department/team, and status as per default workflow.

column2: Attempt to create a job while leaving one mandatory field blank (repeat for each mandatory field)
column3: Job creation is blocked and clear inline validation is shown for each missing required field; no partial job is created.

column2: Enter invalid data format in key fields (e.g., non-numeric in numeric field, invalid date range, special-character-only title)
column3: System prevents save and shows relevant validation messages; user remains on form with entered data retained.

column2: Create a job using boundary values (minimum allowed characters/values)
column3: Job is created successfully when all values are exactly at permitted minimum limits.

column2: Create a job using maximum allowed character limits in long text fields (e.g., job description, requirements)
column3: Form accepts input up to limit and saves successfully without truncation or corruption.

column2: Enter values beyond allowed limits in text/number fields
column3: System blocks submission and clearly indicates the field limit exceeded; no job record is created.

column2: Save a job with scheduling details where opening/closing or start/end dates are logically invalid (end before start)
column3: System prevents save and highlights date logic error with a clear correction message.

column2: Create a job and assign interview panel/recruiter/hiring owner (where applicable)
column3: Job is created and selected assignees are correctly linked and visible in job details.

column2: Use Save/Submit action twice quickly (double click or repeated trigger)
column3: Only one job is created; duplicate records are not generated; user sees a single success outcome.

column2: Create a job, then reopen it from listing/details to verify persistence
column3: All submitted values (title, location, experience, description, skills, assignees, status) are saved accurately and displayed consistently across list and detail views.

column2: Open Job Details from the Jobs list for an existing active job
column3: Job Details page opens successfully and displays the correct job based on the selected row/card.

column2: Verify core job information on Job Details (title, job ID/reference, status, department/team, location)
column3: All core fields are visible and match the job data saved during creation/edit.

column2: Verify job description and key requirement sections on Job Details
column3: Description, responsibilities, and required skills/criteria are displayed completely and in readable format.

column2: Verify ownership/assignment details on Job Details (hiring manager/recruiter/interviewer data if applicable)
column3: Assigned users are shown correctly with the same values configured in the job setup.

column2: Refresh the Job Details page after it is loaded
column3: The same job details reload correctly without data loss, mismatch, or broken sections.

column2: Navigate to another module and return back to the same Job Details
column3: Job Details remains consistent and shows the same latest saved information.

column2: Open Job Details for jobs in different valid states (e.g., draft/open/closed, if supported)
column3: Details page loads successfully for each supported state and shows the correct state-specific information.

column2: Verify candidate/related counts or linked summary widgets on Job Details (if shown)
column3: Counts and summary blocks are displayed and aligned with the job’s current data context.

column2: Open Generate Auto-Source Filter for a job that has all mandatory sourcing inputs completed
column3: Filter generation runs successfully and user sees a generated filter output for that job.

column2: Generate Auto-Source Filter using valid core job criteria (title, skills, experience range, location)
column3: Generated filter includes all provided core criteria accurately and in the correct sections.

column2: Generate Auto-Source Filter for a job with multiple mandatory skills
column3: Filter output correctly includes all selected mandatory skills without dropping any.

column2: Generate Auto-Source Filter for a job with optional/preferred skills configured
column3: Filter clearly separates or reflects preferred criteria without overriding mandatory criteria.

column2: Generate Auto-Source Filter and verify role synonyms/related keywords are included (when feature supports expansion)
column3: Output contains relevant role/skill variants aligned to the original job intent.

column2: Regenerate Auto-Source Filter after updating valid job inputs (e.g., experience or location)
column3: Newly generated filter reflects the latest job changes and replaces stale criteria.

column2: Save/apply the generated Auto-Source Filter to the job
column3: Saved filter persists successfully and is visible when the job is reopened.

column2: Generate Auto-Source Filter for different valid job types (e.g., onsite/remote/hybrid if supported)
column3: Generation succeeds for each supported type and output matches the selected work model criteria.

column2: Generate Auto-Source Filter and proceed to sourcing/search step
column3: The sourcing/search step accepts the generated filter and runs without manual correction.

column2: Generate Auto-Source Filter for an existing job, then reopen later for review
column3: Previously generated/saved filter remains intact, readable, and consistent with the last saved version.

column2: Open a job that already has a previously saved Auto-Source Filter
column3: Saved Auto-Source Filter is fetched and displayed successfully for that job.

column2: Get Saved Auto-Source Filter immediately after saving a newly generated filter
column3: Returned filter content matches exactly what was just saved, with no missing criteria.

column2: Get Saved Auto-Source Filter for a job with full valid criteria set (role, skills, experience, location, work model)
column3: All saved criteria sections are returned and populated correctly in the filter view.

column2: Get Saved Auto-Source Filter for different valid job states where sourcing is allowed (e.g., open/active)
column3: Filter retrieval works consistently and shows the correct saved version for each supported state.

column2: Navigate away and return to the same job’s Saved Auto-Source Filter
column3: Same saved filter is shown consistently, confirming persistence and correct job mapping.

column2: Refresh the page while viewing Saved Auto-Source Filter
column3: Filter reloads successfully with unchanged saved content and readable structure.

column2: Get Saved Auto-Source Filter for multiple jobs that each have their own saved filters
column3: Each job displays only its own saved filter; no cross-job data mixing occurs.

column2: Open Saved Auto-Source Filter and proceed to sourcing/search using that saved filter
column3: Downstream sourcing/search accepts the retrieved saved filter without requiring re-generation.
